# Theme System Documentation

## Overview

The @onlinesales-ai/ui component library uses a custom theme system built on Chakra UI v3. The theme is completely self-contained and doesn't rely on Chakra's default configuration.

## Architecture

### Custom Theme Structure

```
src/theme/
├── theme.js              # Complete theme configuration (9,615 lines)
├── index.js              # Theme exports
├── tokens/               # Base design tokens
│   ├── colors.js
│   ├── fonts.js
│   ├── spacing.js
│   └── ...
├── semantic-tokens/      # Context-aware tokens
│   ├── colors.js
│   └── radii.js
├── recipes/              # Component recipes (simple)
│   ├── button.js
│   ├── badge.js
│   └── ...
└── slot-recipes/         # Component slot recipes (complex)
    ├── card.js
    ├── accordion.js
    └── ...
```

## Tokens vs Semantic Tokens

### Base Tokens

**Raw, concrete values that never change:**

```javascript
// tokens/colors.js
colors: {
  white: { value: "#FFFFFF" },           // Always #FFFFFF
  black: { value: "#09090B" },           // Always #09090B
  blue: {
    500: { value: "#3B82F6" },           // Always this blue
  }
}
```

**Characteristics:**
- ✅ Fixed values
- ✅ Actual color hex codes, pixel values, font names
- ✅ The "foundation" or "building blocks"
- ✅ No context awareness

### Semantic Tokens

**Contextual values that reference tokens and change based on conditions:**

```javascript
// semantic-tokens/colors.js
colors: {
  bg: {
    DEFAULT: {
      value: {
        _light: "{colors.white}",        // White in light mode
        _dark: "{colors.black}",         // Black in dark mode
      }
    },
    muted: {
      value: {
        _light: "{colors.gray.100}",     // Light gray in light mode
        _dark: "{colors.gray.900}",      // Dark gray in dark mode
      }
    }
  }
}
```

**Characteristics:**
- ✅ References base tokens using `{colors.tokenName}` syntax
- ✅ Context-aware (`_light`, `_dark`, `_hover`, etc.)
- ✅ Meaningful, purpose-driven names
- ✅ Used directly in components

### Usage Comparison

```jsx
// ❌ Bad: Using base tokens directly
<Button bg="gray.100" color="gray.900" />
// Problem: Doesn't adapt to dark mode

// ✅ Good: Using semantic tokens
<Button bg="bg.muted" color="fg" />
// Automatically: bg.muted → gray.100 (light) or gray.900 (dark)
```

## Recipes vs Slot Recipes

### Recipes (Simple Components)

**For single-element components:**

```javascript
// recipes/button.js
export const buttonRecipe = defineRecipe({
  className: "chakra-button",
  base: {
    display: "inline-flex",
    borderRadius: "l2",
    cursor: "button",
    // ... all styles apply to THE BUTTON element
  },
  variants: {
    size: {
      md: { h: "10", px: "4" },
      lg: { h: "11", px: "5" }
    },
    variant: {
      solid: { bg: "colorPalette.solid" },
      outline: { borderWidth: "1px" }
    }
  },
  defaultVariants: {
    size: "md",
    variant: "solid",
  }
})
```

**Usage:**
```jsx
<Button size="md" variant="solid">Click Me</Button>
```

All styles apply to **one root element**.

### Slot Recipes (Complex Components)

**For components with multiple sub-parts:**

```javascript
// slot-recipes/card.js
export const cardSlotRecipe = defineSlotRecipe({
  className: "chakra-card",
  slots: ["root", "header", "body", "footer", "title", "description"],
  //      ↑ Each slot is a different element!

  base: {
    root: {                    // Styles for Card wrapper
      display: "flex",
      flexDirection: "column",
    },
    header: {                  // Styles for Card.Header
      paddingTop: "var(--card-padding)",
    },
    body: {                    // Styles for Card.Body
      padding: "var(--card-padding)",
      flex: "1",
    },
    title: {                   // Styles for Card.Title
      fontWeight: "semibold",
    }
  },
  variants: {
    size: {
      md: {
        root: { "--card-padding": "spacing.6" },
        title: { textStyle: "lg" }
      }
    }
  }
})
```

**Usage:**
```jsx
<Card size="md" variant="outline">
  <Card.Header>
    <Card.Title>Card Title</Card.Title>
    <Card.Description>Card description</Card.Description>
  </Card.Header>
  <Card.Body>Content here</Card.Body>
  <Card.Footer>Footer content</Card.Footer>
</Card>
```

Each slot gets **its own styles**.

## Component Styling Strategy

### No Hardcoded Defaults

Components **do NOT** contain hardcoded default props. All defaults are handled by recipes in the theme:

```javascript
// ❌ OLD: Hardcoded defaults
const Button = ({ colorPalette = "primary", variant = "solid", ...props }) => {
  return <ChakraButton colorPalette={colorPalette} variant={variant} {...props} />;
};

// ✅ NEW: Recipe-driven defaults
const Button = (props) => {
  return <ChakraButton {...props} />;
};
```

**Defaults are defined in the theme recipe:**
```javascript
// theme/recipes/button.js
export const buttonRecipe = defineRecipe({
  // ...
  defaultVariants: {
    size: "md",
    variant: "solid",
  }
})
```

### Benefits

1. **Single Source of Truth** - All styling defaults in one place (theme)
2. **Easy Global Changes** - Change defaults without touching component code
3. **Consistent Theming** - All components follow the same theming rules
4. **Cleaner Components** - Components are just thin wrappers
5. **Better Override Control** - Users can override theme recipes easily

## Recipe Structure

### Simple Recipe Example

```javascript
export const badgeRecipe = defineRecipe({
  className: "chakra-badge",
  base: {
    // Base styles applied to all badges
    display: "inline-flex",
    alignItems: "center",
    fontWeight: "medium",
  },
  variants: {
    size: {
      sm: { textStyle: "xs", px: "2", h: "5" },
      md: { textStyle: "sm", px: "2.5", h: "6" },
      lg: { textStyle: "md", px: "3", h: "7" },
    },
    variant: {
      solid: {
        bg: "colorPalette.solid",
        color: "colorPalette.contrast",
      },
      subtle: {
        bg: "colorPalette.subtle",
        color: "colorPalette.fg",
      },
      outline: {
        borderWidth: "1px",
        borderColor: "colorPalette.emphasized",
        color: "colorPalette.fg",
      },
    },
  },
  defaultVariants: {
    size: "md",
    variant: "subtle",
  },
})
```

### Slot Recipe Example

```javascript
export const accordionSlotRecipe = defineSlotRecipe({
  className: "chakra-accordion",
  slots: ["root", "item", "itemTrigger", "itemContent", "itemIndicator"],

  base: {
    root: {
      width: "100%",
    },
    item: {
      borderBottomWidth: "1px",
      borderColor: "border",
    },
    itemTrigger: {
      width: "100%",
      display: "flex",
      alignItems: "center",
      justifyContent: "space-between",
      py: "4",
      px: "2",
      cursor: "button",
      _hover: {
        bg: "bg.muted",
      },
    },
    itemContent: {
      overflow: "hidden",
    },
    itemIndicator: {
      transition: "transform 0.2s",
      _open: {
        transform: "rotate(180deg)",
      },
    },
  },

  variants: {
    variant: {
      plain: {
        item: { borderBottomWidth: "0" },
      },
      enclosed: {
        item: {
          borderWidth: "1px",
          borderRadius: "l2",
          mb: "2",
        },
      },
    },
    size: {
      sm: { itemTrigger: { py: "2", textStyle: "sm" } },
      md: { itemTrigger: { py: "4", textStyle: "md" } },
      lg: { itemTrigger: { py: "6", textStyle: "lg" } },
    },
  },

  defaultVariants: {
    variant: "plain",
    size: "md",
  },
})
```

## Color Palette System

### Using colorPalette

Components support a `colorPalette` prop that dynamically changes colors:

```jsx
// Different color palettes
<Button colorPalette="primary">Primary</Button>
<Button colorPalette="danger">Danger</Button>
<Button colorPalette="success">Success</Button>
```

### Recipe Color References

```javascript
variant: {
  solid: {
    bg: "colorPalette.solid",      // References active palette
    color: "colorPalette.contrast", // Contrast color
    _hover: {
      bg: "colorPalette.solid/90",  // 90% opacity
    },
  },
}
```

### Available Color Palettes

- `primary` - Main brand color (blue)
- `secondary` - Secondary gray colors
- `success` - Green for success states
- `warning` - Orange/yellow for warnings
- `danger` - Red for errors and destructive actions
- `info` - Informational blue
- Standard Chakra colors: `gray`, `red`, `orange`, `yellow`, `green`, `teal`, `blue`, `cyan`, `purple`, `pink`

## Customizing the Theme

### Extending Tokens

```javascript
import { osmosTheme } from "@onlinesales-ai/ui";
import { createSystem } from "@chakra-ui/react";

const customTheme = {
  ...osmosTheme,
  theme: {
    ...osmosTheme.theme,
    tokens: {
      ...osmosTheme.theme.tokens,
      colors: {
        ...osmosTheme.theme.tokens.colors,
        brand: {
          50: { value: "#e3f2fd" },
          500: { value: "#2196f3" },
          900: { value: "#0d47a1" },
        },
      },
    },
  },
};

const customSystem = createSystem(customTheme);
```

### Overriding Recipes

```javascript
const customTheme = {
  ...osmosTheme,
  theme: {
    ...osmosTheme.theme,
    recipes: {
      ...osmosTheme.theme.recipes,
      button: {
        ...osmosTheme.theme.recipes.button,
        defaultVariants: {
          size: "lg",           // Change default size
          variant: "outline",   // Change default variant
        },
      },
    },
  },
};
```

### Adding New Recipes

```javascript
const customTheme = {
  ...osmosTheme,
  theme: {
    ...osmosTheme.theme,
    recipes: {
      ...osmosTheme.theme.recipes,
      myCustomComponent: defineRecipe({
        base: { /* styles */ },
        variants: { /* variants */ },
        defaultVariants: { /* defaults */ },
      }),
    },
  },
};
```

## Best Practices

### 1. Use Semantic Tokens in Components

```jsx
// ✅ Good
<Box bg="bg.muted" color="fg" borderColor="border" />

// ❌ Bad
<Box bg="gray.100" color="black" borderColor="gray.300" />
```

### 2. Let Recipes Handle Defaults

```javascript
// ✅ Good - No hardcoded defaults
const Button = (props) => <ChakraButton {...props} />;

// ❌ Bad - Hardcoded defaults
const Button = ({ variant = "solid", ...props }) => (
  <ChakraButton variant={variant} {...props} />
);
```

### 3. Use colorPalette for Themable Components

```jsx
// ✅ Good - Uses colorPalette system
<Button colorPalette="primary" />

// ❌ Bad - Hardcoded colors
<Button bg="blue.500" color="white" />
```

### 4. Follow Naming Conventions

- **Tokens**: `camelCase` (e.g., `primaryColor`, `baseFontSize`)
- **Semantic Tokens**: `dot.notation` (e.g., `bg.muted`, `fg.subtle`)
- **Recipes**: `componentName + "Recipe"` (e.g., `buttonRecipe`, `cardSlotRecipe`)
- **Slots**: `camelCase` (e.g., `itemTrigger`, `itemContent`)

## Theme File Organization

The consolidated theme file contains:

1. **Conditions** - Responsive breakpoints and state conditions
2. **Base Tokens** - Colors, fonts, spacing, etc.
3. **Semantic Tokens** - Context-aware design tokens
4. **Recipes** - 19 simple component recipes
5. **Slot Recipes** - 54 complex component recipes
6. **Keyframes** - Animation keyframes
7. **Text Styles** - Typography presets
8. **Layer Styles** - Common style combinations
9. **Animation Styles** - Reusable animations
10. **Global CSS** - Global styles

Total: **9,615 lines** of comprehensive theme configuration.

## Migration from Old System

If you're migrating from the old theme system:

### Old Way (Multiple Files)
```javascript
import { colors } from "./tokens/colors";
import { button } from "./recipes/button";
// ... many imports
```

### New Way (Single File)
```javascript
import { osmosTheme } from "@onlinesales-ai/ui";
// Everything included!
```

### Removed Features
- ❌ `defineConfig` function removed
- ❌ Separate token files removed (consolidated)
- ❌ Hardcoded component defaults removed
- ❌ Dependency on Chakra's defaultConfig removed

### Added Features
- ✅ Complete custom theme system
- ✅ Recipe-driven component defaults
- ✅ Consolidated theme configuration
- ✅ Full Chakra UI v3 compatibility
